# Implementation Plan: Read Node Configuration

**Branch**: `004-read-node-config` | **Date**: February 19, 2026 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/004-read-node-config/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command. See `.specify/templates/commands/plan.md` for the execution workflow.

## Summary

Read configuration values from all LCC nodes using the Memory Configuration Protocol (address space 0xFD). Display progress indication during batch reading ("Reading [Node Name] config... X%") and show current values in the Miller Columns details panel when users select configuration elements. All configuration values are read upfront after node refresh completes, cached in memory, and displayed from cache during navigation. Users can manually refresh individual values via a "Refresh Value" button.

**Technical Approach**: Leverage existing Memory Configuration Protocol implementation in `lcc-rs/src/protocol/memory_config.rs` (build_read/parse_read_reply methods). Add new Tauri commands to read configuration values by navigating CDI structure to get memory addresses, sending datagram read requests, and parsing typed responses. Extend Miller Columns store with config value cache and progress state. Integrate into existing node refresh workflow with progress UI updates.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Rust 2021 (backend via lcc-rs), TypeScript 5.x (frontend)  
**Primary Dependencies**: lcc-rs (LCC protocol), Tauri 2 (desktop framework), SvelteKit (reactive UI)  
**Storage**: In-memory cache (Map<NodeId, Map<ElementPath, TypedValue>>), no persistence  
**Testing**: cargo test (Rust backend), vitest (TypeScript frontend), integration tests with mock nodes  
**Target Platform**: Windows/Linux/macOS desktop (Tauri 2 multi-platform)  
**Project Type**: Desktop application (Tauri + SvelteKit hybrid architecture)  
**Performance Goals**: Read single element value <2s, complete 3-node network ≤15s, support 100+ elements per node  
**Constraints**: <5% error rate under normal network conditions, UI must remain responsive during reads (cancellable), values cached to avoid redundant network I/O  
**Scale/Scope**: Support 3-10 nodes typical, 10-100 config elements per node, handle replicated groups (up to 16 instances)

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

### I. Rust 2021+ Development ✅ PASS
- Backend uses existing lcc-rs library (Rust 2021 edition)
- New Tauri commands follow async/await patterns from existing code
- Uses Result types for error handling (no unwrap() in production)
- No new Rust dependencies required (reuses tokio, lcc-rs)
- Integration: app/src-tauri/src/commands/cdi.rs (extends existing)

### II. Cargo-Based Development Environment ✅ PASS
- Uses existing Cargo setup in app/src-tauri/Cargo.toml
- No build system changes needed
- Dependencies already declared in workspace

### III. Test-Driven Development ⚠️ REQUIRES TEST PLAN
- **ACTION REQUIRED**: Must add tests before implementation:
  - Unit tests for value parsing (int/string/eventid/float) in commands/cdi.rs
  - Integration tests with mock Memory Configuration Protocol responses
  - Property tests: parse(format(value)) == value for all types
  - Frontend tests for progress calculation and cache updates
- **Enforcement**: PR must include test coverage for new commands
- **Reference**: Follow pattern from lcc-rs/tests/protocol/ for protocol tests

### IV. LCC Protocol Correctness ✅ PASS
- Uses existing Memory Configuration Protocol implementation (lcc-rs/src/protocol/memory_config.rs)
- Address space: 0xFD (Configuration) per **TN-9.7.4.2 §2.1**
- Memory addresses from CDI per **TN-9.7.4.1 §3.2** (offset calculation)
- Datagram protocol per **TN-9.7.3.2** (already tested in lcc-rs)
- **References**: 
  - TN-9.7.4.2 (Memory Configuration Protocol)
  - TN-9.7.4.1 (Configuration Description Information)
  - Existing implementation: lcc-rs/src/discovery.rs read_cdi() method as pattern

### V. UX-First Design ✅ PASS
- Progress indication with human-readable node names: "Reading [Node Name] config... X%"
- SNIP data priority cascade: user_name > user_description > model_name > node_id
- Values formatted appropriately: dotted hex for event IDs, decimal for ints, UTF-8 for strings
- Automatic reading on refresh reduces manual steps (upfront batch read)
- Manual "Refresh Value" button for verification workflow
- Clear error messages: "Failed to read value: node not responding"

### VI. TCP-Only Focus ✅ PASS
- Uses existing TCP connection infrastructure (no transport changes)
- Reuses AppState connection management from app/src-tauri/src/state.rs
- Network I/O via existing LccConnection methods

### VII. Event Management Excellence ✅ PASS (indirect)
- Feature enables reading event ID configuration values (8-byte event IDs)
- Event IDs displayed in dotted hexadecimal format: `01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08`
- Supports event configuration workflows (view current event assignments)
- Foundation for future event modification features

**GATE VERDICT**: ✅ **CONDITIONAL PASS** - Implementation can proceed once test plan is documented below. Re-evaluate after Phase 1 design to ensure test coverage is adequate.

**Test Plan Summary** (satisfies TDD requirement):
1. **Backend Unit Tests** (app/src-tauri/src/commands/cdi.rs):
   - Test value parsing for each element type (int 1/2/4/8 bytes, string, eventid, float)
   - Test address calculation: segment.origin + element.offset
   - Test error handling: node timeout, invalid response, malformed data
2. **Backend Integration Tests** (app/src-tauri/tests/):
   - Mock Memory Configuration Protocol responses
   - Test multi-element batch reading
   - Test replicated group expansion
3. **Frontend Unit Tests** (app/src/lib/):
   - Test progress calculation (current/total percentage)
   - Test config value cache updates
   - Test type-specific formatters (formatEventId, formatInt, formatString)
4. **End-to-End Tests** (Tauri test harness):
   - Test full refresh flow with simulated nodes
   - Test cancellation during config reading
   - Test error recovery (skip failed node, continue)

---

### Post-Phase 1 Re-Evaluation (February 19, 2026)

After completing Phase 1 design artifacts (data-model.md, contracts/, quickstart.md), all constitution gates have been re-evaluated:

**I. Rust 2021+ Development** ✅ PASS (unchanged)
- Design follows async/await patterns documented in quickstart.md
- Error handling uses Result types throughout
- Type safety maintained in ConfigValue enum and helper functions

**II. Cargo-Based Development Environment** ✅ PASS (unchanged)
- No new dependencies added
- Existing lcc-rs and Tauri infrastructure sufficient

**III. Test-Driven Development** ✅ PASS (improved with detailed test examples)
- quickstart.md Section "Testing Strategy" provides comprehensive unit test examples
- Backend tests: `test_parse_int_value_1_byte`, `test_parse_eventid_value`, `test_snip_priority_cascade`
- Integration tests: `test_read_all_config_values_with_mock_node` with mock connection
- Frontend tests: config value cache and progress calculation tests
- Test coverage aligns with test plan summary above

**IV. LCC Protocol Correctness** ✅ PASS (unchanged)
- data-model.md cites TN-9.7.4.2 §2.1 for address space 0xFD
- data-model.md cites TN-9.7.4.1 §3.2 for address calculation
- quickstart.md implementation examples use Memory Configuration Protocol correctly
- Contract schemas validate protocol compliance

**V. UX-First Design** ✅ PASS (enhanced with detailed UI patterns)
- quickstart.md documents progress indicator UI with percentage display
- SNIP priority cascade implemented consistently (get_node_display_name helper)
- Value formatting functions provide human-readable output (formatConfigValue)
- User workflow documented: refresh → batch read → navigate → display

**VI. TCP-Only Focus** ✅ PASS (unchanged)
- Design reuses existing TCP connection from AppState
- No transport layer modifications

**VII. Event Management Excellence** ✅ PASS (unchanged)
- ConfigValue::EventId type supports 8-byte event IDs
- quickstart.md shows event ID formatting: dotted hex display
- Enables future event configuration features

**FINAL VERDICT**: ✅ **FULL APPROVAL** - All constitution requirements satisfied. Design is complete and ready for implementation phase (Phase 2: /speckit.tasks).

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
app/
├── src-tauri/                 # Rust backend (Tauri 2)
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── commands/
│   │   │   ├── cdi.rs         # ← MODIFY: Add read_config_value, read_all_config_values commands
│   │   │   └── discovery.rs   # Reference: follows refresh_all_nodes pattern
│   │   ├── lib.rs             # ← MODIFY: Register new commands in invoke_handler
│   │   └── state.rs           # Reference: AppState connection management
│   ├── Cargo.toml
│   └── tests/                 # ← ADD: Integration tests for config reading
│       └── config_reading.rs
├── src/                       # SvelteKit frontend
│   ├── lib/
│   │   ├── api/
│   │   │   ├── cdi.ts         # ← MODIFY: Add readConfigValue, readAllConfigValues wrappers
│   │   │   └── types.ts       # ← MODIFY: Add ConfigValue, ConfigValueMap types
│   │   ├── components/
│   │   │   └── MillerColumns/
│   │   │       ├── DetailsPanel.svelte   # ← MODIFY: Display config values
│   │   │       └── NodesColumn.svelte    # Reference: loading state pattern
│   │   └── stores/
│   │       └── millerColumns.ts          # ← MODIFY: Add configValues cache, readProgress state
│   └── routes/
│       └── +page.svelte       # ← MODIFY: Integrate config reading into refresh flow
└── package.json

lcc-rs/                        # LCC protocol library (reference only, no changes)
└── src/
    ├── protocol/
    │   └── memory_config.rs   # Reference: build_read(), parse_read_reply() usage
    ├── cdi/
    │   └── mod.rs             # Reference: Element types with offsets
    └── discovery.rs           # Reference: read_cdi() method as pattern

tests/
└── integration/               # ← ADD: End-to-end tests with simulated nodes
    └── config_reading_e2e.rs
```

**Structure Decision**: This is a Tauri desktop application with clear backend/frontend separation. Backend (Rust) handles LCC protocol operations via lcc-rs library and exposes Tauri commands. Frontend (SvelteKit/TypeScript) provides reactive UI with component-based architecture. Miller Columns store pattern already established for state management. New functionality extends existing command structure (commands/cdi.rs) and store (millerColumns.ts) rather than creating new modules, maintaining architectural consistency.
